# Functional Specification — Home Page as Product Narrative

**Document:** Build-ready spec for `docs/index.html`, generated by `cartrack-ai-design-system/portal/docs/build_portal.py` → `body_home()` (~line 828) and, where noted, `render_shell()` (~line 547) for shared CSS.
**Builds on:** `home-page-visual-redesign-spec.md` (already implemented — ink hero, Montserrat display type, route-line motif, corrected token colours). That spec fixed the *look*; this spec changes the *content model*. No visual-language regressions intended — new sections reuse the classes that spec introduced (`.path`, `.steps`/`.stepc`, `.idxgrid`, `.rule`) rather than inventing new ones.
**Constraint (explicit):** No changes to `design-system/` (tokens, components, AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md) or to `guides/index.html`'s role as the canonical, full-depth explanation. This spec only changes what `body_home()` renders.
**Status:** Implemented 2026-07-21 — Builds 1–3 in one pass. Open questions resolved per spec recommendations: text link only (no screenshot embed), proof block reads vibe-tests/results/ live at build time, path copy condensed to one line + guide link.

---

## 1. Goal

Today `index.html` is a docs-portal front door: hero → five-card index → one warning callout. It tells a visitor where to click but never actually explains what this thing is, why it exists, or why they should trust it before they click anything. This spec turns the home page into a short product narrative — problem, what-it-is, proof, audience, how-it-works, then the index — so a first-time visitor (designer, developer, or someone evaluating whether to adopt this at all) understands the product before they navigate away from the page that's supposed to sell it.

## 2. Non-goals

- No change to the sidebar nav, filter box, or collapsible tree.
- No change to `guides/index.html` (the "How to start" long-form article) as the canonical source for full instructions, core concepts, and rules. The home page **teases**, it does not duplicate at length — see §5.
- No change to any component/pattern/foundation page.
- No new external dependencies (fonts, scripts, CDNs) beyond what `home-page-visual-redesign-spec.md` already introduced (self-hosted Montserrat).
- No fabricated metrics. Every number on the page must trace to a real file (`tokens.json`, `components/`, `vibe-tests/results/`) — this page documents a system whose own first rule is "don't invent"; it would undercut the product to invent stats about itself. See §8.

## 3. Inputs

| Source | What this spec reads from it |
|---|---|
| `guides/index.html` content (`body_guides()`, ~line 878) | Source paragraphs for "why it's built this way," the `#paths` cards, the `#quick-start` steps — condensed, not copied verbatim, onto the home page. Guide keeps the full versions. |
| `design-system/tokens/tokens.json` | Component count, token-tier claim — already piped into `n_comps` today. |
| `design-system/vibe-tests/results/<latest>/` + `aggregate.mjs` | Real pass-rate evidence for the proof block, if a run exists (see §8). |
| `home-page-visual-redesign-spec.md`'s CSS additions | `.route`, `.statstrip`, `.path`/`.paths`, `.steps`/`.stepc`, `.rule`, `.idxgrid` — reused, not replaced. |

## 4. Content principle: home is the trailer, guide is the feature

Every new section below is 2–4 lines plus one link into the matching guide anchor (`#why`, `#paths`, `#quick-start`, `#concepts`). This is a deliberate constraint, not an oversight: the guide page already contains the full version of almost everything a "product homepage" would want to say (see §3 — `body_guides()` already has "why it's built this way," full path cards with prompt examples, and quick-start steps). Writing a second, independent long-form version on the home page would create two copies of the same claims that can drift out of sync. Instead, home-page copy is written fresh and short, deliberately in a different register (pitch, not instructions), and always points down into the guide for depth. If a reviewer ever finds a factual claim on the home page that isn't also traceable to the guide or a real file, that's a bug in this spec, not a style choice.

## 5. Proposed page structure

Order below; **NEW** = doesn't exist today, **CONDENSED** = shorter version of content that already exists on `guides/index.html`, **KEPT** = unchanged, **MOVED** = relocated from another page, **DEMOTED** = still present, lower visual priority.

1. **Hero — KEPT + one addition.** Headline, paragraph, route line, stat strip all unchanged. Add one short positioning line directly under the paragraph, above the route line: *"Not a component library on its own — the actual Fleet Portal's tokens and components, packaged so an AI agent can build with them without touching production code."* Plain text, body sans, no new markup beyond a `<p class="tagline">`.
2. **Why this exists — NEW, condensed from guide `#why`.** 2–3 lines naming the actual problem (design systems drift from the product they document; AI agents with no constraints invent their own visual language) before the solution is asserted. Links to `guides/index.html#why` for the full "lean AI-optimized design system" explanation.
3. **What it is — NEW, condensed from guide `#inside`.** Not the full 8-row file table from the guide — a 3–4 item version reusing `.filemap`-style rows (already styled, used on the guide's downloads/quick-start context): tokens, components, patterns, agent instructions. One line each. Links to `guides/index.html#inside` for the full table.
4. **Proof — NEW, replaces the stat strip's implicit "trust me."** See §8 for the data-sourcing rule. Concept: the same four stats already in the hero's `.statstrip`, but each one gets a one-line "how do you know" answer directly beneath it (e.g. "27 components — each one mapped prop-for-prop against the real fleetapp-web source, not designed from scratch"). Reuses `.rule` styling, not a new component.
5. **Pick your path — CONDENSED + MOVED-in-spirit.** `.paths`/`.path` markup already exists and is styled (orange/ink/blue left rules) but only renders on `guides/index.html#paths` today. Add a shorter version to the home page: same three cards, same left-rule colour coding, but drop each card's prompt-example block (that detail stays on the guide) down to the `<h4>` + one line. Card links to `guides/index.html#paths` for the full version with try-it prompts.
6. **How it works — NEW, condensed from guide `#quick-start`.** Three short steps (connect the folder → agent reads `CLAUDE.md`/`AGENTS.md` → agent builds from real tokens/components), reusing `.steps`/`.stepc` markup already defined for the guide's two-step quick start. This becomes the home page's first appearance of the getting-started motif — it does not exist on `index.html` today (verified: the current `body_home()` has the hero's route line and stat strip, but no `.steps` block; that pattern currently lives only on `guides/index.html`).
7. **See it — NEW.** One link, visually promoted (not just a card in the index grid): "See every component rendered — no setup" → `resources/preview.html`. If build effort allows, embed a short static screenshot of `component-library-preview.html` instead of only a text link (no iframe — keeps the page load-independent of another HTML file's own script/CSS; a screenshot is a build-time artifact, not a live embed). Flagged as optional in §10.
8. **What's here (index grid) — DEMOTED, not removed.** Same five cards, same copy. Moves from "the whole page's content" to "wayfinding after the pitch" — visually smaller heading treatment (`h5`-weight "More in this portal" instead of `h2` "What's here"), positioned after §5–7, immediately before the closing warning callout.
9. **Warning callout — KEPT verbatim,** same position (end of page).

## 6. What this explicitly removes or changes from today

- The stat strip stays in the hero (unchanged) **and** gets a second, expanded appearance in the new Proof block (§5.4) — these are not the same content twice: the hero strip is the number, the proof block is the number's evidence.
- "What's here" goes from `h2`/first-content-block to `h5`/late-page. Its five cards, links, and copy are untouched.
- Nothing currently on the home page is deleted.

## 7. Technical approach

All changes are inside `body_home()` (~line 828). No new top-level CSS components are needed — every new section reuses a class already defined in `render_shell()` from the prior visual spec:

- §5.1 tagline: one `<p class="tagline">` — add a two-line rule to `render_shell()` (`.tagline{color:#c9cdd2;font-size:14.5px;margin-top:10px}` — matches the existing hero paragraph colour, not a new colour).
- §5.2–5.3 (Why / What it is): plain `<section>` + `<h2>` + `<p class="sub">`, identical pattern to every other portal page — no new CSS.
- §5.4 (Proof): reuse `.statstrip` for the numbers, `.rule` for the one-line evidence underneath each — both classes already exist and are already used elsewhere (`.rule` on the guide's "core concepts" and "rules" sections).
- §5.5 (Paths teaser): reuse `.paths`/`.path`/`.who` verbatim from `render_shell()`; the only markup difference from the guide's version is omitting the `.prompt` block per card.
- §5.6 (How it works): reuse `.steps`/`.stepc` verbatim from `render_shell()`, three `.stepc` divs instead of the guide's two.
- §5.7 (See it): one `.livelink`-styled anchor (class already exists, used on component pages) — no new CSS. Screenshot embed, if built, is a static `<img>` pointing at a build-time-generated asset (e.g. `assets/preview-thumb.png`), not a live iframe — needs a one-time capture step added to whatever process updates the portal, flagged in §10.
- §5.8 (Index grid demotion): same `.idxgrid`/`.idxcard` markup, wrapped in a `<h5>`-styled label instead of `<h2>` — add one CSS rule for a `.subhead` variant if `h5` alone doesn't read as a section break; otherwise no new CSS.

Because every new section is a `<section>` inside the existing `.inner` wrapper, page-width, spacing (`margin-bottom:56px` on `section`), and responsive behaviour are inherited for free — no new breakpoints needed.

## 8. Proof block — real-data sourcing rule, with graceful degradation

Checked against what actually exists today: `design-system/vibe-tests/results/smoke1/` currently has **two** evaluated prompts (`a11y-orange-trap`, `fab-gap-trap`), both `"success": true` — a smoke run, not a full aggregate pass rate. `aggregate.mjs` exists to summarize a full iteration but hasn't been run at scale yet.

Rule for the generator: the Proof block's evidence line for the "agent behavior" claim must read from whatever `vibe-tests/results/` actually contains at generation time, with three tiers:

1. **No results directory / empty:** omit any agent-behavior proof line entirely. Don't show a stat with no data behind it.
2. **Smoke-only (fewer than, say, 10 evaluated prompts, or a single iteration):** show it honestly — *"Smoke-tested against 2 adversarial prompts (accessibility trap, undocumented-pattern trap) — both correctly flagged the gap instead of inventing a fix."* No percentage, because a 2-prompt sample presented as a percentage overstates confidence.
3. **Full aggregate run available:** show the real pass rate from `aggregate.mjs`'s output, with the iteration name and prompt count (e.g. "94% pass rate across 18 prompts, run 2026-08-01") so it's falsifiable and dated, not a permanent-looking claim.

The other three Proof lines (components mapped to real code, one tokens source of truth, zero setup steps) are static facts already true today and don't need tiering — just accurate phrasing sourced from `tokens.json`/`components/` counts already computed elsewhere in `build_portal.py` (`n_comps`, etc.).

## 9. What this spec deliberately does not touch

- `guides/index.html` content — stays the canonical full version of everything the home page now teases.
- Sidebar navigation, filter, mobile drawer behaviour.
- Any component, pattern, or foundation page.
- `tokens.json`, `components/`, `AGENTS.md`/`CLAUDE.md` — the product itself, not its homepage.
- The visual language established by `home-page-visual-redesign-spec.md` (colours, type, radius scale, route-line motif) — extended, not replaced.

## 10. Open questions before build

1. **Screenshot embed (§5.7):** worth the one-time capture step, or is a text link sufficient for v1? Recommend shipping v1 with the text link only and adding the screenshot as a fast-follow once the rest of the page is validated — avoids blocking this spec on a build-process change.
2. **Proof block tone once a full vibe-test run exists:** should the home page auto-update from the latest `aggregate.mjs` output every regeneration, or should someone consciously refresh the cited number when a new run completes? Recommend the generator reads live at build time (same pattern as `n_comps`) so it can't silently go stale — but flagging it since it's a new category of "home page reads from a test-results folder," not just from tokens/components.
3. **Path card copy (§5.5):** condensing the existing guide copy to one line per card risks losing the thing that makes each path concrete (the try-it prompt). Recommend drafting actual copy and reviewing side-by-side with the guide's version before this ships, rather than approving the structure blind.
4. **Section count / page length:** this adds five new sections (Why, What it is, Proof, Paths teaser, How it works) to a page that was previously three blocks. Worth a scroll-length sanity check once built — if it reads as too long, the first cut candidate is folding "Why" and "What it is" into a single combined block rather than dropping proof or paths.

## 11. Rollout

1. **Build 1 (P1):** §5.1 tagline, §5.2 Why, §5.3 What it is, §5.8 index-grid demotion. Lowest-risk, no data-tiering logic, establishes the new narrative order.
2. **Build 2 (P2):** §5.5 Paths teaser, §5.6 How it works — reuses existing CSS verbatim, adds the two sections that most directly answer "who is this for" and "what do I do next."
3. **Build 3 (P3):** §5.4 Proof block with the tiered vibe-test sourcing from §8 — deferred slightly later because it's the one section with real data-availability logic to get right.
4. **Deferred, not in this spec:** §5.7's screenshot embed (open question 1); any equivalent narrative restructuring of other portal pages (out of scope — this spec is home-page-only, matching the precedent set by `home-page-visual-redesign-spec.md` §7).

## 12. Acceptance criteria

- `design-system/` (tokens, components, AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md, vibe-tests) requires zero changes to ship this spec — the generator only reads from it.
- `guides/index.html` is unchanged; every condensed home-page section links to the matching guide anchor.
- No new CSS component classes — every new section is built from `.path`, `.steps`/`.stepc`, `.rule`, `.statstrip`, `.idxgrid`, `.livelink`, all already defined in `render_shell()`.
- The Proof block never displays a percentage or count that isn't read live from `vibe-tests/results/` at generation time; with zero or few results it degrades to the tiers in §8, never to a placeholder or invented figure.
- Regenerating the portal after any change is still exactly one `build_portal.py` run.
- No new WCAG AA violations — new sections reuse already-audited colour/type combinations from the prior visual spec; no new colour introduced.
